msgs.text:
I read the prompt and the 35-turn proposal log as an external research record. The prompt fixes the tame-format syntax: sigma,tau are distinguished, the tame relation tau^sigma=tau^2 is implicit, later generators must lie in the pro-2 core, and exponents are reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log leaves only the dynamic tame-inertia candidate alive; the scalar, plain-ŌÄ2, and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 candidates were refuted by finite tame or index-two tests.

The still-live candidate is variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2 and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1.

The constants check out as ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ő∑ is irrelevant because that exponent is only applied to ŌĄ, whose finite images have odd order.The most important audit correction is that the latest prior turns still had the two reciprocal formulas backwards. The sign-cover Fox coefficient should beDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j=1‚ąítőĪa 1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít,not (1‚ąítőĪa)/(1‚ąít). Here t is the deck involution of the sign double cover and ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1 is a nontrivial tame-inertia character. Since 1‚ąítőĪa is a unit and 1‚ąít is radical in the 2-adic sign-cover group algebra, Da is radical. Therefore the coefficient from x‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. This salvages the intended conclusion: nontrivial odd tame-inertia blocks contribute no 2-primary Smith torsion.The trivial tame-inertia elimination is also the reciprocal of what some prior summaries stated. The mod-2 relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0,s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf).

For f=2a m with m odd, s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm). If f is even, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís, and the valid substitution is

Y=P(T)X+YňČ,P(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís.

With this P,h(T)=1+P(T)+P(T‚ąí1),and for any nontrivial f-th root ő∂,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1.

Thus h(ő∂)=0 would force ő∂2s+1=1. But 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), hence gcd(f,2s+1)=1. So the trivial block is nonsingular.My new structural computation refines Turn 35‚Äôs ‚Äúodd-order rigidity‚ÄĚ point. LetőĒe,f=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČe=1,ŌÉňČf=1,ŌÉňČŌĄňČŌÉňČ‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©,e¬†odd,¬†e‚ą£2f‚ąí1.

Write f=2a m with m odd. Then

H=‚ü®ŌĄňČ,ŌÉňČ2a‚ü©is a normal odd Hall subgroup of őĒe,f, andőĒe,f/H‚ČÉC2a.For a finitely generated pro-2 kernel U, the IA-automorphism groupAut1 (U)=ker(Aut(U)‚ÜíAut(U/ő¶U))is pro-2. Therefore any ambiguity in lifting a fixed Frattini action has trivial restriction to H, by Schur‚ÄďZassenhaus at finite 2-quotient level. More precisely, after conjugacy, the residual deformation lives inH1(C2a,Aut1(U)H).So the odd Hall action is indeed rigid, but the remaining even-Frobenius problem is not merely the pure unramified problem: H-fixed higher IA pieces can contain genuinely mixed tame terms.I checked this explicitly in the first mixed case (e,f)=(3,2), where őĒ3,2‚ČÉS3, H=C3, and P=C2. At the first IA stage modulo U3, useL1‚ČÉF2[S3]‚äēTb‚äēTS with marked S fixed, and use the candidate-compatible quadratic relationq=g‚ąąS3‚ąĎXg[2]+[b,S].ThenL2 ‚ČÉ(L1[2]‚äē‚ąß2L1)/‚ü®q‚ü©has dimension 35. The marked first-stage deformation module is

M=HomC3(F2[S3]‚äēTb,L2),and direct F2 -row reduction gives dimker(1+P‚ą£M)=44,rank(1+P‚ą£M)=39,hencedimF2H1(C2,M)=5.For comparison, the same calculation in the pure unramified quadratic case (e,f)=(1,2) gives dimension 3, matching the prior marked ambiguity count after fixing S. Thus the mixed tame case has two additional first-stage Frobenius-action ambiguity directions already at the U/U3 level. I am not claiming these integrate without higher corrections, and I am not claiming the live candidate fails. The point is that Turn 35‚Äôs rigidity reduction must not be read as ‚Äúonly unramified 2a -tests remain‚ÄĚ; mixed even tame quotients have extra H-invariant deformation directions that a final arithmetic comparison must control.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I settled the reciprocal-formula issue: the correct sign-cover coefficient is Da=(1‚ąít)/(1‚ąítőĪa), and the correct trivial-block substitution is P(T)=(1+T‚ąís)/(1+T). I also refined the formation gap: after killing the odd Hall ambiguity, residual even-Frobenius deformations lie in H1(C2a,Aut1(U)H). In the first mixed case (e,f)=(3,2), the first IA ambiguity space has dimension 5, not the unramified-quadratic dimension 3.

GAP: The candidate is still not a proved presentation of GQ2. The remaining proof must identify the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered C2a -Frobenius action on the H-fixed IA tower with arithmetic. Mixed tame even-f cases have extra deformation directions not detected by abelianization, cup product, sign kernel, or pure unramified tests.

NEXT: Compute the candidate‚Äôs actual class in the five-dimensional (e,f)=(3,2) U/U3 ambiguity space, then compare it with explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic through U/U5. That should reveal whether the two mixed-tame directions are forced by arithmetic or require another correction term.[BREAKTHROUGH]
